message.content:
You are an independent fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for a production-hardening pass on the hc-assay repo at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Be adversarial and skeptical — your job is to find any way the fix is wrong, incomplete, or its regression test fails to discriminate.

FINDING J-002 (HIGH):
Operator full display name (e.g. git user.name "[REDACTED]") left in cleartext in committed transcripts — distinct PII the email/username rules don't cover; appears in git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose. Fix: _display_name_patterns() derives names from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES env + GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/GIT_COMMITTER_NAME + git config user.name + git log -1 --format=%an; builds a whitespace-flexible regex with NO leading \b (name often glued to a JSON-escaped \n) but a trailing \b (protect "Joneses"), >=4-char guard, de-duped. Applied in _scrub after username redaction. Test: test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name. ALSO verify the working tree of the repo actually has zero raw occurrences of the operator name in transcripts/ (grep) — a passing unit test does not prove the committed corpus is clean.

The complete code diff for all pass-7 fixes is at /tmp/pass7_code.diff (read it). The full files are under /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. You may run the test suite (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>) — NOTE: use "python -m pytest", not bare "pytest", or the tests/ package won't import.

Judge ONLY finding J-002. Decide:
- appropriate: does the fix address the real root cause with a sound approach (not a band-aid, not the wrong layer)?
- complete: is there any remaining gap, AND does the regression test actually FAIL if the fix is reverted (i.e. does it discriminate)? If you can, reason about or test revert-discrimination.
Return CONFIRMED only if both are true. Return CONCERN if appropriate but with a real residual gap. Return REJECTED if the fix is wrong or the test is non-discriminating. List concrete concerns.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll review finding J-002. Let me start by reading the diff and the relevant code.

diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index ed0873a..9e1e25c 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -32,7 +32,8 @@ jobs:
           # core lock carries only the `dev` extra (no backends), preserving the ADR-0006 guard.
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-core.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Lint (ruff)
         run: ruff check src tests
       - name: Format check (ruff)
@@ -77,7 +78,8 @@ jobs:
           # release within a pyproject range could run during the real-backend tests (#F-004).
           python -m pip install --upgrade pip
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
       - name: Type-check against real backend types (mypy)
         run: mypy
       - name: Verify service containers are reachable (no silent skip)
@@ -136,6 +138,12 @@ jobs:
           uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock
           diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-core.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-core.regen.lock) \
             || { echo "::error::requirements-core.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile pyproject.toml --extra dev --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-core.lock"; exit 1; }
+          # the build-backend lockfile (hatchling etc.) backs the hash-pinned --no-build-isolation
+          # editable install (#J-003) and must stay in sync.
+          printf 'hatchling\neditables\n' > /tmp/build-reqs.in
+          uv pip compile /tmp/build-reqs.in --universal --generate-hashes -o /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock
+          diff <(grep -v '^#' requirements-build.lock) <(grep -v '^#' /tmp/requirements-build.regen.lock) \
+            || { echo "::error::requirements-build.lock is stale — run: uv pip compile <(printf 'hatchling\\neditables\\n') --universal --generate-hashes -o requirements-build.lock"; exit 1; }
       - name: pip-audit (reproducible known-vulnerability scan)
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
@@ -188,9 +196,10 @@ jobs:
           # live `pip install -e .[all]` resolution that could pull different versions than the
           # lockfile pins (#F-013). Advisory monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot.
           pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.lock
-          pip install --no-deps -e .
+          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements-build.lock  # hashed build backend (#J-003)
+          pip install --no-build-isolation --no-deps -e .
           python -m pip install pip-licenses==5.5.5  # pinned for reproducible license output (#G-022)
-          pip-licenses --format=json --with-system > licenses.json
+          pip-licenses --format=json > licenses.json  # scan the dep set, not system tooling (#J-006)
           python scripts/license_gate.py licenses.json
       - name: Upload license report
         if: always()
diff --git a/requirements-build.lock b/requirements-build.lock
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..4b1395d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/requirements-build.lock
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@

+hatchling==1.30.1 \
+    --hash=sha256:161eacafb3c6f91526e92116d21426369f2c36e98c36a864f11a96345ad4ee31 \
+    --hash=sha256:eee4fd45357f72ebb3d7a42e5d72cfb5e29ed426d79e8836288926c4258d5f2e
+    # via -r /tmp/build-reqs.in
+packaging==26.2 \
+    --hash=sha256:5fc45236b9446107ff2415ce77c807cee2862cb6fac22b8a73826d0693b0980e \
+    --hash=sha256:ff452ff5a3e828ce110190feff1178bb1f2ea2281fa2075aadb987c2fb221661
+    # via hatchling
+pathspec==1.1.1 \
+    --hash=sha256:17db5ecd524104a120e173814c90367a96a98d07c45b2e10c2f3919fff91bf5a \
+    --hash=sha256:a00ce642f577bf7f473932318056212bc4f8bfdf53128c78bbd5af0b9b20b189
+    # via hatchling
+pluggy==1.6.0 \
+    --hash=sha256:7dcc130b76258d33b90f61b658791dede3486c3e6bfb003ee5c9bfb396dd22f3 \
+    --hash=sha256:e920276dd6813095e9377c0bc5566d94c932c33b27a3e3945d8389c374dd4746
+    # via hatchling
+trove-classifiers==2026.6.1.19 \
+    --hash=sha256:ab4c4ec93cc4a4e7815fa759906e05e6bb3f2fbd92ea0f897288c6a43efd15b3 \
+    --hash=sha256:c5132b4b61a829d11cfbd2d72e97f20a45ed6edb95e45c5efdeb5e00836b2745

diff --git a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
index 465013e..fb2ff89 100755
--- a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
@@ -73,7 +73,52 @@ def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
     return re.compile(re.escape(name))
 
 
+def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
+    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
+
+    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
+    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
+    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
+    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
+    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
+    over-redacting common words.
+    """
+    names: list[str] = []
+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
+    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
+    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
+    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
+    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
+    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
+    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
+        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
+        if v:
+            names.append(v)
+    try:
+        import subprocess
+
+        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
+            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
+            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
+                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
+        pass
+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
+    seen: set[str] = set()
+    for n in names:
+        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
+        seen.add(n)
+        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
+        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
+        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
+        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
+        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
+    return pats
+
+
 _USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
+_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
 
 
 def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
@@ -88,6 +133,10 @@ def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
     # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
     if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
         text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
+    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
+    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
+    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
+        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
     return text
 
 
@@ -104,13 +153,18 @@ def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
         # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
         # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
         # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
-        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
-        try:
-            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
-        except UnicodeDecodeError:
-            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
+        #
+        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
+        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
+        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
+        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
+        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
+        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
+        # present) is copied verbatim.
+        if b"\x00" in raw:
+            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
         else:
-            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
+            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
         if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
             pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
         else:
diff --git a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
index f00cd07..01fdff2 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py
@@ -65,7 +65,19 @@ class MlflowExperimentTracker:
         exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
         if exp is not None:
             return str(exp.experiment_id)
-        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
+        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
+        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
+        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
+        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
+        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
+        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
+        try:
+            return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
+        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize the concurrent-create race to a re-fetch
+            exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
+            if exp is not None:
+                return str(exp.experiment_id)
+            raise
 
     def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
         client = self._client()
diff --git a/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py b/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
index 79964bf..106212f 100644
--- a/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
+++ b/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py
@@ -140,10 +140,20 @@ def test_t3_hung_subprocess_is_cancelled_not_leaked(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
     assert _t.monotonic() - t0 < 5  # cancelled promptly near the 0.3s inner timeout, not hung
 
 
+def _assert_transient(excinfo):
+    # #J-005: RateLimitError and PermanentReasoningError both SUBCLASS ReasoningError, so
+    # `pytest.raises(ReasoningError)` does NOT discriminate a transient mapping from a rate-limit
+    # or permanent one. Assert the exception is the transient class itself, not a decisive subclass.
+    assert not isinstance(excinfo.value, (RateLimitError, PermanentReasoningError)), (
+        f"expected a transient ReasoningError, got {type(excinfo.value).__name__}"
+    )
+
+
 def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
     fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()
+    _assert_transient(ei)
 
 
 def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
@@ -167,8 +177,9 @@ def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
 
 def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
     fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()
+    _assert_transient(ei)  # a 500 must be transient, NOT permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
 
 
 def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
@@ -202,8 +213,9 @@ def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
 def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
     # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
     fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
-    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
+    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError) as ei:
         _run()
+    _assert_transient(ei)  # transport raise must be transient, not permanent/rate-limit (#J-005)
 
 
 def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
diff --git a/tests/test_observability.py b/tests/test_observability.py
index 84831f6..64b6aee 100644
--- a/tests/test_observability.py
+++ b/tests/test_observability.py
@@ -123,6 +123,32 @@ def test_bootstrap_logs_warning_on_setup_failure(monkeypatch, caplog):
     assert any("setup failed" in r.message for r in caplog.records)
 
 
+def test_experiment_id_survives_concurrent_create_race(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
+    # #J-004: check-then-create is a TOCTOU — on the first-time race, create_experiment raises
+    # RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS for the loser. _experiment_id must re-fetch the now-existing
+    # experiment instead of propagating (which would leave run_id=None and drop all metrics).
+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", f"sqlite:///{tmp_path / 'x.db'}")
+
+    class _Exp:
+        experiment_id = "exp-7"
+
+    class RacingClient:
+        def __init__(self):
+            self.calls = 0
+
+        def get_experiment_by_name(self, name):
+            # first call (the check) sees nothing; after the failed create, the winner's exists
+            self.calls += 1
+            return None if self.calls == 1 else _Exp()
+
+        def create_experiment(self, name):
+            raise RuntimeError("RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS: another writer created it")
+
+    t = MlflowExperimentTracker()
+    client = RacingClient()
+    assert t._experiment_id(client) == "exp-7"  # re-fetched, not raised
+
+
 def test_start_run_terminates_run_when_param_logging_fails(monkeypatch, tmp_path):
     # #F-023: if create_run succeeds but log_param fails, the run must be terminated FAILED, not
     # left orphaned in RUNNING state. Mock the MLflow client to exercise the failure path without
diff --git a/tests/test_scripts.py b/tests/test_scripts.py
index 510b00f..b66eceb 100644
--- a/tests/test_scripts.py
+++ b/tests/test_scripts.py
@@ -123,6 +123,39 @@ def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
     assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"
 
 
+def test_scrub_fails_closed_on_invalid_utf8_bytes():
+    # #J-001: a stray non-UTF-8 byte must NOT disable redaction for the whole file. The scrubber
+    # decodes errors='replace' and classifies binary by a NUL byte, so a secret next to an invalid
+    # byte is still scrubbed (the pass-6 strict-decode fix had made this fail OPEN).
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_failclosed")
+    raw = b'{"out":"[REDACTED] caf\xe9 /Users/[REDACTED]/x"}'  # invalid \xe9
+    text = raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+    out = cap._scrub(text)
+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # secret scrubbed despite the bad byte
+    assert "somebody" not in out
+
+
+def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_file_with_invalid_utf8(tmp_path):
+    # #J-001 end-to-end: a .jsonl with an invalid byte is still scrubbed on copy, not copied raw.
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_fc2")
+    src = tmp_path / "t.jsonl"
+    src.write_bytes(b'{"k":"[REDACTED] \x80 end"}')  # invalid \x80
+    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "t.jsonl"
+    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
+    out = dst.read_bytes()
+    assert b"abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out and b"[REDACTED]" in out
+
+
+def test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name(monkeypatch):
+    # #J-002: the operator's full display name (from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES / git user.name) is redacted,
+    # not just the email — distinct PII that leaked beside redacted emails.
+    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "Ada Lovelace")
+    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_name")
+    out = cap._scrub("Author: Ada Lovelace <[REDACTED]>; also Ada   Lovelace in prose")
+    assert "Ada Lovelace" not in out and "Ada   Lovelace" not in out  # flexible whitespace too
+    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out  # email still scrubbed
+
+
 def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
     # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
     # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
diff --git a/tests/test_supply_chain.py b/tests/test_supply_chain.py

+++ b/tests/test_supply_chain.py

+def test_ci_pins_build_backend_and_disables_live_build_isolation():
+    # #J-003: the editable install must use the hashed build backend (requirements-build.lock) with
+    # --no-build-isolation, so hatchling isn't fetched live/un-hashed from PyPI (an unscanned,
+    # non-reproducible build-time code-exec window).

+    assert "hatchling==" in build and "--hash=sha256:" in build

40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED]    # The Claude project-dir SLUG (e.g. -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc): redact the username segment
53	    # regardless of length (#H-018) — the bare-token rule below skips short handles to avoid
54	    # over-redacting common words, but the slug form is unambiguous PII at any length.
55	    re.compile(r"(-(?:Users|home)-)[^-\s/\"']+"),  # -Users-[REDACTED]- → -Users-[REDACTED]-
56	]
57	
58	
59	def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
60	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
61	
62	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
63	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
64	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
65	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
66	    """
67	    try:
68	        name = Path.home().name
69	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
70	        return None
71	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
72	        return None
73	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
74	
75	
76	def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
77	    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
78	
79	    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
80	    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
81	    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
82	    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
83	    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
84	    over-redacting common words.
85	    """
86	    names: list[str] = []
87	    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
88	    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
89	    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
90	    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
91	    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
92	    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
93	    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
94	        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
95	        if v:
96	            names.append(v)
97	    try:
98	        import subprocess
99	
100	        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
101	            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
102	            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
103	                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
104	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
105	        pass
106	    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
107	    seen: set[str] = set()
108	    for n in names:
109	        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
110	            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
111	        seen.add(n)
112	        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
113	        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
114	        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
115	        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
116	        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in n.split()) + r"\b"))
117	    return pats
118	
119	
120	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
121	_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = _display_name_patterns()
122	
123	
124	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
125	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
126	        if pat.groups == 2:
127	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
128	        elif pat.groups == 1:
129	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
130	        else:
131	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
132	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
133	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
134	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
135	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
136	    # Redact the operator's full display name too (#J-002) — distinct PII the username/email rules
137	    # leave in cleartext (git author strings, pyproject authors=, prose).
138	    for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES:
139	        text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
140	    return text
141	
142	
143	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
144	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
145	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
146	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
147	
148	
149	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
150	    try:
151	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
152	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
153	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
154	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
155	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
156	        #
157	        # FAIL CLOSED on redaction (#J-001): classify binary by an actual binary SIGNAL (a NUL
158	        # byte), NOT by UTF-8 validity. A transcript routinely embeds arbitrary subprocess stdout
159	        # (latin-1 dumps, truncated multibyte) inside JSONL strings; gating on strict decode meant
160	        # a single stray byte routed the WHOLE file to the verbatim branch and re-leaked it (the
161	        # CV-O-1 fix had turned an always-redact path into a fail-open one). Decode with
162	        # errors="replace" so redaction ALWAYS runs on text; only a genuinely binary blob (NUL
163	        # present) is copied verbatim.
164	        if b"\x00" in raw:
165	            data = raw  # genuinely binary — nothing to scrub
166	        else:
167	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
168	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
169	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest

Those "[REDACTED]@protonmail" occurrences (without .com) are inside prior grep-command strings echoed in transcripts — these are partial-email fragments that the email regex correctly doesn't match (no TLD), and they're self-referential search commands. Those are a separate, arguably out-of-scope artifact. The important point for J-002 is the display name.

Now let me confirm the revert-discrimination: would the test fail if the fix is reverted? The test sets `ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES=Ada Lovelace` then checks `_scrub` redacts it. But `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES` is a module-level global computed at import time — the monkeypatch.setenv happens after import, so the env var wouldn't be picked up by the already-computed `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES`. 17:def _load(rel: str, name: str):
28:    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts")
45:    gate = _load("scripts/license_gate.py", "license_gate_fd")
===
"""Guards for repo scripts + examples (pass 3: #F-051, #F-048).

These exercise non-package files (scripts/, examples/) that the wheel never ships but that run
on every commit (transcript capture) or are copied by users (the example). Where a test targets
a file's *content* it reads THAT file and asserts both the corrected form and the absence of the
stale/insecure form (the artifact-vs-test fidelity rule).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path

_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent


def _load(rel: str, name: str):
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, _ROOT / rel)
    assert spec and spec.loader
    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
    return mod


def test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars():
    # #F-051: a bearer token containing base64 chars (+ / =) must be FULLY redacted, not split at
    # the first such char leaving a trailing fragment in the transcript.
    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts")
    text = "Authorization: [REDACTED]"
    scrubbed = cap._scrub(text)
    assert "[REDACTED]" in scrubbed
    # no recognizable token fragment survives
    for frag in ("eyJhbGc", "payload/signature", "/signature", "iOiJIUzI1NiJ9"):
        assert frag not in scrubbed, f"token fragment {frag!r} leaked past redaction"


def test_license_gate_closes_the_report_file(tmp_path):
    # #F-050: the report must be opened via a context manager so the fd is closed deterministically.
    # Discriminating: intercept the module's open() and capture the file handle; after main()
    # returns, assert it is .closed. A bare json.load(open(...)) leaves the handle open (closed

content.text:
`_load` does `exec_module` fresh each call, so the monkeypatched `ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES=Ada Lovelace` IS picked up at module exec time, meaning `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES` is recomputed with the env. Good — the test is correctly structured. The monkeypatch.setenv runs before `_load`, so the fresh module sees it. The test discriminates: reverting the fix removes `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES` and the `for pat in _DISPLAY_NAME_RES` loop, so "Ada Lovelace" would survive → test fails. Confirmed discriminating.

Let me verify the test ordering (setenv before _load) and run the full script test file to ensure no regressions.

# simulate revert: empty the display-name patterns

content.text:
Confirmed: with the fix reverted (`_DISPLAY_NAME_RES = []`), "Ada Lovelace" survives → test would fail. The test discriminates.

Now the critical assessment. Let me consolidate:

**Appropriate:** Yes — deriving from multiple sources (env, git config, git log author) and scrubbing in `_scrub` after username redaction is the right layer. The no-leading-`\b`/trailing-`\b` reasoning is sound. The >=4-char guard and de-dup are reasonable.

**Complete:** Here is the real residual gap. The finding's explicit secondary requirement: "verify the working tree actually has zero raw occurrences of the operator name in transcripts/." The FULL name "[REDACTED]" has zero occurrences (good). BUT the operator's bare first name "[REDACTED]" appears 63+ times in committed prose ("[REDACTED] approves Charter amendments", "[REDACTED] builds and governs the infrastructure"), and the fix by design only redacts the full multi-token name, never the first name alone. This is distinct operator PII left in cleartext in the committed corpus — exactly the "prose" leak vector J-002 names.

One could argue the first name is "the same identity" not the "full display name". But the finding's worked example IS this operator, and the verification clause asks for zero raw occurrences of "the operator name." A first name that uniquely re-identifies the operator across 63 prose instances is a real residual gap. This is a CONCERN, not a rejection — the full-name fix is correct and discriminating, but the corpus is not clean of the operator's identity.

Let me double check whether "[REDACTED]" alone would even be catchable by this approach (it wouldn't, since names list contains "[REDACTED]" full string, 2-token pattern requires both tokens).

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
import importlib.util
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('cap','scripts/capture_transcripts.py')
m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(m)
print('patterns:', [p.pattern for p in m._DISPLAY_NAME_RES])
print('[REDACTED] alone scrubbed?:', '[REDACTED]' in m._scrub('[REDACTED] approves Charter amendments'))
print('[REDACTED] scrubbed?:', '[REDACTED]' in m._scrub('committed by [REDACTED] today'))
"

content.content:
patterns: ['[REDACTED]\\s+[REDACTED]\\b']
[REDACTED] alone scrubbed?: False
[REDACTED] scrubbed?: True

content.text:
Confirmed. The pattern is only `[REDACTED]\s+[REDACTED]\b`. The full name is scrubbed; the bare first name "[REDACTED]" (63+ committed prose occurrences) is not.

I have enough to judge. Summary of my findings:

- The fix is appropriately layered and the multi-source derivation (env vars, git config, git log author) correctly handles this node where local `git config user.name` is empty but the commit author is "[REDACTED]".
- The regression test `test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name` is correctly structured (setenv before fresh `_load`/`exec_module`) and I empirically confirmed it discriminates — reverting `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES` leaves "Ada Lovelace" in the output, failing the test.
- The full name "[REDACTED]" has zero raw occurrences in the committed `transcripts/` tree. The full email is redacted.
- RESIDUAL GAP: the operator's bare first name "[REDACTED]" appears 63+ times in committed transcript prose ("[REDACTED] approves Charter amendments", "[REDACTED] builds and governs the infrastructure"). This is uniquely re-identifying operator PII in the exact "prose" vector J-002 names, and the fix by design cannot catch it. The finding's own verification clause ("zero raw occurrences of the operator name in transcripts/") is satisfied only if "the operator name" means the full string; under the natural reading it is not clean.

input.rationale:
The fix is soundly designed and at the right layer. _display_name_patterns() derives candidate names from ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES, GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/GIT_COMMITTER_NAME, `git config user.name`, and `git log -1 --format=%an`; the multi-source approach is justified because on this node local `git config user.name` is empty yet `git log` author is "[REDACTED]". The whitespace-flexible regex with no leading \b but trailing \b (protecting "Joneses") plus the >=4-char guard and de-dup are all reasonable. Applied in _scrub after username redaction.

The regression test test_scrub_redacts_operator_display_name DOES discriminate. _load() does a fresh importlib exec_module, and monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES","Ada Lovelace") runs before _load, so _DISPLAY_NAME_RES is recomputed with the env present. I empirically simulated the revert (setting _DISPLAY_NAME_RES=[]) and confirmed "Ada Lovelace" survives, so the test would fail if the fix is reverted. Full test_scripts.py suite: 11 passed.

Working-tree verification: the FULL name "[REDACTED]" has zero raw occurrences in committed transcripts/ (2575 tracked files), and the full operator email is redacted. So far so good.

RESIDUAL GAP (why CONCERN not CONFIRMED): the operator's bare first name "[REDACTED]" appears 63+ times in committed transcript prose — e.g. "[REDACTED] approves Charter amendments and tier completion", "[REDACTED] builds and governs the infrastructure", "[REDACTED] is still the sole Charter approver". The fix's only active pattern is `[REDACTED]\s+[REDACTED]\b`; it scrubs the full name but cannot and does not scrub the standalone first name (confirmed: _scrub('[REDACTED] approves...') yields no [REDACTED]). This is uniquely re-identifying operator PII sitting in cleartext in the exact "prose" vector J-002 calls out, and the finding's own secondary requirement — "verify the working tree actually has zero raw occurrences of the operator name in transcripts/" — is satisfied only under a narrow reading where "the operator name" means the literal full string. Under the natural reading, the committed corpus is not clean of the operator's identity. The full-name fix is correct, but the leak it was meant to close is only partially closed.
concerns: Bare first name '[REDACTED]' remains in cleartext 63+ times across committed transcripts/ prose (e.g. '[REDACTED] approves Charter amendments', '[REDACTED] builds and governs the infrastructure'). The fix only matches the full '[REDACTED]\s+[REDACTED]\b' pattern and by design cannot redact a standalone first name, so this uniquely-identifying operator PII survives in the exact prose vector J-002 names.

The finding's explicit verification clause ('zero raw occurrences of the operator name in transcripts/') passes only for the literal full string; the operator remains trivially re-identifiable from the corpus, so the corpus is not actually clean.
Even on re-capture, the corrected scrubber would still leave all 63+ '[REDACTED]' first-name prose occurrences untouched — the residual is structural, not just a stale-corpus artifact.

Fragmentary email handle '[REDACTED]@protonmail' (no TLD) appears inside echoed grep-command strings in transcripts and is not matched by the email regex (needs a TLD). Lower severity / arguably out of J-002 scope, but it is another raw operator-identity fragment in the committed tree.

Minor: the >=4-char and trailing-\b guards do not bound false positives for a common-word full name (e.g. an operator literally named 'Will Banks' would over-redact), but that is a tolerable tradeoff and not a J-002 gap.

content.text:
I've completed my adversarial review of finding J-002.

**Verdict: CONCERN**

The fix is appropriately designed and the regression test discriminates (empirically confirmed — reverting `_DISPLAY_NAME_RES` leaves "Ada Lovelace" unredacted, failing the test). The full name "[REDACTED]" has zero raw occurrences in the committed `transcripts/` tree, and the full operator email is redacted.

However, there is a real residual gap: the operator's bare first name "[REDACTED]" remains in cleartext 63+ times in committed transcript prose (e.g. "[REDACTED] approves Charter amendments", "[REDACTED] builds and governs the infrastructure"). The fix's only active pattern is `[REDACTED]\s+[REDACTED]\b`, which cannot match a standalone first name. This is uniquely re-identifying operator PII in the exact "prose" vector J-002 names, and the structural nature means even a re-capture would not clean it. The finding's verification clause is satisfied only under a narrow reading of "the operator name" as the literal full string.

Relevant file: `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py` (lines 76-140).
